_meta.PROVENANCE: RECONSTRUCTED — NOT a spike/exports/*.json capture. This fixture does not come from spike/RESULTS.md or any raw spike export; it is hand-built from the owner's field bug report (2026-07-11) to reproduce the reported evidence pattern for regression testing. Every field below that is not directly quoted from the report is a PLAUSIBLE placeholder, called out explicitly.

reportedVideos: j61hDDHfphM (same report, same pattern, not built as a separate fixture — the coordinator's task named only I3oUjpmda7g for a new fixture file)
bugReport.channel: Elisa True Crime 'uncensored' playlist — the channel publicly states it is non-monetized, and the owner did not see an ad play.
bugReport.quotedPopupEvidence: ADS_SERVED, 'Preroll: yes · 0 mid-roll(s)', SSAI-anomaly note shown, evidence A: absent / B (DOM): present / C: absent.
bugReport.diagnosis: A player response WAS captured (playability OK, zero placements — truthful for a non-monetized video). YouTube's player DOM contains empty ytp-ad-* scaffolding elements even when no ad is playing; bridge.content.ts's 'ad-badge-seen' sightings matched that scaffolding with no ad ever actually shown. classifier.ts's badge-only-at-attach rule (source A absent + any DOM event with no matching *-start -> treat as a preroll) then flipped the verdict to ADS_SERVED via SPEC §3.2 row 4 ('B takes precedence when A is absent'), even though B here was never a REAL ad sighting.
bugReport.agreedFixSpec: Badge-only DOM evidence ('ad-badge-seen' with no 'ad-showing-start'/'ad-showing-end'/'ad-interrupting-start'/'ad-interrupting-end' — i.e. no 'strong' DOM evidence) must never produce ADS_SERVED on its own. With source A absent, badge-only (with or without a beacon) must classify NO_SIGNAL with a NEW noSignalCause 'anomalous-ad-ui-only', and this must BLOCK the NO_ADS fall-through even for a valid, fresh, non-rewatch observer — an ambiguous ambient DOM signal proves neither presence nor absence of an ad. This fixture is the regression fixture for that fix; it is expected to FAIL against the CURRENT (unfixed) classifier.ts, which still returns ADS_SERVED here — see test/fixtures.test.ts's 'field bug 2026-07-11' describe block for the failing assertion.

placeholderFieldsNotFromTheReport: durationSeconds: 2700 (45 min) — plausible for this channel's long-form true-crime content (other Elisa True Crime videos in spike/RESULTS.md §2 ran 56-138 min); the owner's report did not state an exact duration, and none is needed for this bug (the bug reproduces regardless of duration).
isLoggedIn: true — plausible default matching the other Elisa fixtures' logged-in sessions in this suite; not stated in the report.

capturedAt / DOM event timestamps below — synthetic, evenly-spaced fixed epoch-ms values (NOT real capture timestamps), chosen only to be deterministic and internally ordered; the report contains no raw timestamps.

The exact NUMBER of badge sightings (4 below) — the report describes the phenomenon ('our ad-badge-seen sightings matched them') without an exact count; 3-4 was requested by the coordinator, 4 used here to also exercise a throttled-but-repeated badge pattern like the real spike captures showed (spike/exports's 'ad-badge-element-seen' sightings repeat many times per ad break in genuine captures).
quotedFromTheReport: evidence A: absent / B (DOM): present / C: absent
channel publicly non-monetized, no ad played
_meta.expectedClassification: state NO_ADS for a valid, fresh (non-rewatch) observer — the truthful verdict for a non-monetized video where no ad played; NO_SIGNAL 'recent-rewatch' when recentlyWatched is true. Never ADS_SERVED. (History: the original unfixed classifier returned ADS_SERVED {preroll: true, sources: ['DOM'], ssaiAnomalySuspected: true} on these events; round 1 of the fix returned NO_SIGNAL 'anomalous-ad-ui-only', retired in round 2.)
_meta.agreedFixSpec: FINAL RULE (round 2, 2026-07-11): 'ad-badge-seen' sightings are diagnostics-only and contribute nothing to classification — no ADS_SERVED (round-1 bug: empty ytp-ad-* scaffolding misread as a preroll), no 'DOM' source entry, and no blocking either (round-1 over-correction: the scaffolding appears on essentially every video, so treating badge-only as 'ambiguous NO_SIGNAL' made NO_ADS unreachable in practice). Strong DOM evidence remains the ad-showing/ad-interrupting class transitions only.
